metadata.title: Awesome UMI
metadata.data_sources: Public-only (papers, project pages, GitHub repos, press releases, curated articles and videos)
metadata.scope: UMI-style handheld and hand/wearable interfaces for tactile and manipulation motion data collection (excludes dedicated egocentric video platforms; see docs/SCOPE.md)

products.full_name: Universal Manipulation Interface
products.name: UMI
products.key_innovation: Relative-trajectory action representation, inference-time latency matching

specs.cost_breakdown: Gripper $73 (BOM, 3D printed) + GoPro Hero 9 with accessories $298
specs.significance: Progenitor; de facto comparison baseline for follow-on research

products.full_name: Fast-UMI: A Scalable and Hardware-Independent Universal Manipulation Interface
products.name: Fast-UMI
products.key_innovation: No SLAM calibration required; decoupled hardware design

specs.tracking_details: RealSense T265 on-chip VIO (replaces UMI Visual SLAM)

products.full_name: MV-UMI: A Scalable Multi-View Interface for Cross-Embodiment Learning
products.name: MV-UMI
products.key_innovation: Third-person view reduces OOD gap; ~47% gain on scene-understanding tasks

products.full_name: UMIGen: A Unified Framework for Egocentric Point Cloud Generation and Cross-Embodiment Robotic Imitation Learning
products.name: Cloud-UMI / UMIGen
products.key_innovation: Records RGB and point cloud jointly; no Visual SLAM; visibility-aware optimization

products.full_name: UMI-3D: Extending Universal Manipulation Interface from Vision-Limited to 3D Spatial Perception
organization: University of Science and Technology of China
products.name: UMI-3D
products.key_innovation: Adds 3D spatial perception while preserving UMI low cost and portability

specs.compatibility: Fully mechanically compatible with UMI; custom top cover only

products.full_name: LEGATO: Cross-Embodiment Imitation Using a Grasping Tool
organization: UT Austin Human Centered Robotics Lab
products.name: LEGATO
products.key_innovation: Cross-embodiment morphology support; same tool mountable on robot side

products.full_name: ViTaMIn: Learning Contact-Rich Tasks Through Robot-Free Visuo-Tactile Manipulation Interface
products.name: ViTaMIn
products.key_innovation: No teleop robot required; visuo-tactile integration

products.full_name: ViTaMIn-B: A Reliable and Efficient Visuo-Tactile Bimanual Manipulation Interface
products.name: ViTaMIn-B
products.key_innovation: Eliminates SLAM drift; bimanual 6DoF unified via Quest 3

specs.tracking_details: Meta Quest 3 controllers for 6-DoF bimanual pose

products.full_name: Touch in the Wild: Learning Fine-Grained Manipulation with a Portable Visuo-Tactile Gripper
products.name: Touch in the Wild
products.key_innovation: In/outdoor in-the-wild visuo-tactile sync; joint representation learning via mask reconstruction encoder

products.full_name: FlexiTac: A Low-Cost, Open-Source, Scalable Tactile Sensing Solution for Robotic Systems
products.name: FlexiTac

products.full_name: TacUMI: A Multi-Modal Universal Manipulation Interface for Contact-Rich Tasks
products.name: TacUMI

specs.tracking_details: HTC Vive Tracker (VR controller / tracker, 6-DoF pose)
specs.compatibility: UMI compatible; preserves robot arm–EE layout
specs.validation: 90%+ segmentation accuracy on cable routing task

products.full_name: exUMI: Extensible Robot Teaching System with Action-aware Task-agnostic Tactile Representation
products.name: exUMI

specs.tracking_details: Meta Quest 3 + AR MoCap + magnetic rotary encoder (AS5600)

products.full_name: TAMEn: Tactile-Aware Manipulation Engine for Closed-Loop Data Collection in Contact-Rich Tasks
products.name: TAMEn

products.full_name: FreeTacMan: Robot-free Visuo-Tactile Data Collection System for Contact-rich Manipulation
products.name: FreeTacMan

specs.tactile_setup: dual visuo-tactile sensors (gel + camera + programmable lighting)
specs.user_study_result: Outperforms ALOHA and UMI on completion rate, efficiency, and CPUT scores

products.full_name: ForceMimic: Force-Centric Imitation Learning with Force-Motion Capture System for Contact-Rich Manipulation
products.name: ForceMimic / ForceCapture

specs.validation: Zucchini peeling 5 min vs teleop 13+ min

products.full_name: DexCap: Scalable and Portable Mocap Data Collection System for Dexterous Manipulation
products.name: DexCap (Stanford)

specs.tracking_details: Wrist via 3× SLAM tracking cameras on hand mounts; finger motion via Rokoko EMF mocap gloves (not onboard VIO)

products.full_name: DexCap by DexRobot (commercial product)
products.name: DexCap (DexRobot)

specs.accessories: Hub, 10000mAh×2 power bank, 3000mAh×4 battery, wireless receivers×3, gloves (S/M/L×2), arm straps

products.full_name: DexUMI: Using Human Hand as the Universal Manipulation Interface for Dexterous Manipulation
products.name: DexUMI

specs.tactile_sensor_details: Exoskeleton-mounted tactile sensors (electromagnetic on XHand, FSR on Inspire Hand) for policy input; operator haptic feedback is separate

products.full_name: RealDexUMI: A Wearable Universal Manipulation Interface for Dexterous Robot Learning
products.name: RealDexUMI
products.key_innovation: Shared dexterous end-effector module with in-hand vision and fingertip tactile sensing; zero-gap collection-to-deployment data via matched observations, contacts, and hand actions

specs.tactile_sensor_details: Fingertip-integrated tactile sensors on all five fingers
specs.tracking_details: Dedicated 6-DoF tracker rigidly mounted on hand module (sub-mm dynamic tracking; max error <1 mm, avg <0.1 mm); palm-side isomorphic teleoperation glove provides joint commands separately
specs.interface: palm-side isomorphic teleoperation glove; retargeting-free real-time joint mapping
supported_robots: Cross-embodiment transfer (3 embodiments in paper eval)

products.full_name: DEXOP: A Device for Robotic Transfer of Dexterous Human Manipulation
products.name: DEXOP

specs.validation_result: 160 DEXOP + 40 TeleOP demos → 0.513±0.032 cumulative success on 6-stage bulb installation

products.full_name: AirExo: Low-Cost Exoskeletons for Learning Whole-Arm Manipulation in the Wild
products.name: AirExo

specs.performance: 3 min teleop + diverse in-the-wild = 20+ min teleop alone equivalent

products.full_name: AirExo-2: Scaling up Generalizable Robotic Imitation Learning with Low-Cost Exoskeletons
products.name: AirExo-2
products.key_innovation: Visual adaptor, pseudo-robot demo conversion, RISE-2 policy integration

products.full_name: DexWild: Dexterous Human Interactions for In-the-Wild Robot Policies
products.name: DexWild

specs.wrist_tracking: External monocular camera + ArUco markers on glove
specs.performance: 68.5% success in unseen environments (~4x baseline), 5.8x cross-embodiment

specs.limitations: Mostly quasi-static tasks; environment-specific fine-tuning required

products.full_name: GenRobot DAS Gripper
products.name: DAS Gripper
products.key_innovation: UMI-inspired handheld multimodal collector (vision, tactile, audio, IMU, magnetic encoder); MCAP output; cloud compression to ~2% of original size

specs.tracking_details: Magnetic encoder (gripper opening) + 6-axis IMU @ 200 Hz; BEV-odometry trajectory reconstruction
specs.main_camera: Triple fisheye RGB (1600×1300, 30 Hz, global shutter, 180° FOV)

products.full_name: GenRobot DAS Dex
products.name: DAS Dex
products.key_innovation: 23-DoF wearable dexterous hand collector; sub-millimeter fingertip trajectories; 3D tactile at 0.05 N / 1 mm; 200 Hz output

specs.tracking_details: 3 mm magnetic encoder; wrist-mounted camera (150° FOV); ego-hand spatial sync <1 ms

products.full_name: GenRobot DAS Fingers
products.name: DAS Fingers
products.key_innovation: Bionic two-finger handheld collector; 220 mm max opening; millimeter-level trajectory; automatic voice annotation

specs.tracking_details: Self-developed BEV-odometry + 6-axis IMU @ 200 Hz; ego-hand multi-device sync
specs.main_camera: Fisheye RGB (1600×1300, 30 Hz, global shutter, >150° FOV)

products.full_name: GenRobot DAS Controller
products.name: DAS Controller
products.key_innovation: Arm-mounted multimodal end effector homologous with DAS collection devices; 448-point tactile @ 1 mm; one-step gripper swap

specs.endeffector: interchangeable hard, soft, and tactile grippers (0–103 mm)

sources.title: Pika: Fast Data Collector Solution for Embodied Intelligence (Hackster.io, 2025-03-07)

products.full_name: AgileX Robotics Pika Sense (handheld data collector; Pika / Pika Pro suite)
products.name: Pika Sense
products.key_innovation: Commercial UMI-style handheld collector: human demonstrations without a robot on-site; pairs with Pika Station for mm-level pose; homologous Pika Gripper for deployment on PiPER and other arms

specs.tracking_details: HTC Vive Tracker (VR controller / tracker, 6-DoF pose); Pika Station base for spatial anchoring; ±1.5 mm accuracy; 120 Hz pose output
specs.main_camera: Depth (87°×58° FOV, 90 FPS) + wide-angle (200° diagonal FOV, 90 FPS); 9-axis IMU @ 100 Hz

specs.nexarm_specs: ESP32+AT32 dual-chip, K230 vision (6 TOPS), 65kg·cm magnetic encoder bus servos

products.full_name: Robotiq Tactile Sensor Fingertips (TSF-85)
products.name: Robotiq TSF-85
products.key_innovation: Capacitive fingertip pads (28 taxels) with 1000 Hz vibration and 3-axis IMU for Physical AI data collection on 2F grippers

products.full_name: YUBI: Yielding Universal Bidigital Interface
products.name: YUBI
products.key_innovation: Open-source bidigital (two-finger) dexterous stack: glove + DYNAMIXEL gripper + stationary/portable collection rigs

specs.license_notes: CERN Open Hardware Licence v2 - Weakly Reciprocal (CERN-OHL-W)
specs.tracking_details: Meta Quest Touch Plus controllers (6-DoF wrist pose); AS5601 magnetic angle sensors for finger joints; ESP32C6
specs.significance: Toyota FRC open-source dexterous data collection and teleop baseline (2026 baseline release)

products.name: DM-DataClaw
products.full_name: DM-DataClaw Data Collection Gripper
products.key_innovation: Handheld parallel-jaw data collection gripper with vision-based tactile sensors (110,000 sensing units, 120Hz); used alongside DM-DataDex to build the Daimon-Infinity omni-modal dataset.

sources.title: DAIMON Robotics Wants to Give Robot Hands a Sense of Touch (IEEE Spectrum)

specs.dataset: Daimon-Infinity (10,000+ hours open-sourced, 1M+ downloads on ModelScope)

products.notes: Two-finger handheld gripper for parallel-jaw manipulation data collection. Part of Daimon's 3D (Device-Data-Deployment) strategy alongside DM-DataDex.

products.name: DM-DataDex
products.full_name: DM-DataDex Dexterous Hand Data Collection Device
products.key_innovation: Five-finger glove-type dexterous data collection device with vision-based tactile sensors (110,000 sensing units, 120Hz); used alongside DM-DataClaw to build the Daimon-Infinity omni-modal dataset.

sources.title: DAIMON Robotics Wants to Give Robot Hands a Sense of Touch (IEEE Spectrum)

specs.tracking_details: HTC Vive Tracker (VR controller / tracker, 6-DoF wrist pose)
specs.dataset: Daimon-Infinity (10,000+ hours open-sourced, 1M+ downloads on ModelScope)

products.notes: Five-finger glove form factor for dexterous manipulation data collection. Part of Daimon's 3D (Device-Data-Deployment) strategy. Equipment used to build the Daimon-Infinity dataset in collaboration with Google DeepMind, Northwestern University, NUS, etc.

sources.title: ACT-1: A Robot Foundation Model Trained on Zero Robot Data

products.full_name: Sunday Robotics Skill Capture Glove
products.name: Skill Capture Glove
products.key_innovation: UMI-inspired wearable co-designed with Memo robot 3-finger gripper (shared geometry and sensor layout); wide-FOV camera hat; crowdsourced in-home data via Memory Developers; Skill Transform pipeline (~90% glove-to-robot conversion)

specs.companion_hardware: Wide-FOV camera hat (matches Memo head camera)

products.notes: Co-founder Cheng Chi authored the UMI paper. Device is proprietary; distributed to paid Memory Developers for in-home chore demonstrations.

products.full_name: DexViTac: Collecting Human Visuo-Tactile-Kinematic Demonstrations for Contact-Rich Dexterous Manipulation
organization: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
products.name: DexViTac
products.key_innovation: Portable visuo-tactile-kinematic collection with Manus mocap gloves, HIT LongLin-96 fingertip sensors, GoPro, and T265; kinematics-grounded tactile representation learning

specs.tactile_sensor_name: HIT LongLin-96 (8×16 taxel, 0–20 N, 1000 Hz sampling)

products.notes: Paper states flexible pressure-sensitive fingertip sensors; sensor technology type not explicitly named in primary source.

products.full_name: DexEXO: A Wearability-First Dexterous Exoskeleton for Operator-Agnostic Demonstration and Learning
products.name: DexEXO
products.key_innovation: Wearability-first linkage exoskeleton with passive data-capture hand for hardware-level visual and kinematic alignment; hand lengths 140–217 mm without per-user calibration

products.full_name: DEX-Mouse: A Low-cost Portable and Universal Interface with Force Feedback for Data Collection of Dexterous Robotic Hands
products.name: DEX-Mouse
products.key_innovation: Calibration-free 6-DoF handheld interface with kinesthetic force feedback (<USD 150 BOM); forearm-mounted robot-hand configuration for robot-aligned data without morphological retargeting

specs.contact_sensing: Motor torque values as proprioceptive inputs (no external tactile sensor)

products.notes: Paper describes open-source hardware and software stack; repository URL not listed in primary source.

products.full_name: Exo-ViHa: A Cross-Platform Exoskeleton System with Visual and Haptic Feedback for Efficient Dexterous Skill Learning
products.name: Exo-ViHa
products.key_innovation: 3D-printed forearm exoskeleton with interchangeable dexterous hands; T265 pose, wrist camera, and motion-capture glove; passive haptic feedback via exoskeleton contact

specs.hand_tracking: Motion capture glove (type not specified in paper)

products.notes: Haptic feedback is passive force sensation through exoskeleton contact, not instrumented tactile sensing.

products.full_name: OmniUMI: Towards Physically Grounded Robot Learning via Human-Aligned Multimodal Interaction
products.name: OmniUMI
products.key_innovation: Handheld multimodal UMI capturing RGB, depth, trajectory, marker-based tactile images, internal grasping force, and external wrench with bilateral gripper feedback

specs.tactile_format: Tactile images with marker displacements (vision-based tactile)

products.full_name: ActiveUMI: Robotic Manipulation with Active Perception from Robot-Free Human Demonstrations
products.name: ActiveUMI
products.key_innovation: Portable VR teleoperation kit with robot grippers on Meta Quest 3s controllers; records operator head motion for active egocentric perception during bimanual in-the-wild collection

specs.main_camera: Fisheye wrist cameras + HMD front cameras

products.full_name: HoMMI: Learning Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation from Human Demonstrations
products.name: HoMMI
products.key_innovation: UMI grippers augmented with egocentric sensing for whole-body mobile manipulation; cross-embodiment hand-eye policy with embodiment-agnostic 3D visual representation

products.full_name: ManiWAV: Learning Robot Manipulation from In-the-Wild Audio-Visual Data
products.name: ManiWAV
products.key_innovation: UMI parallel-jaw gripper with embedded piezoelectric contact microphone ('ear-in-hand') for synchronous in-the-wild audio-visual demonstration collection

specs.audio: Piezoelectric contact microphone via GoPro mic port (48 kHz); synced with 60 Hz RGB

products.notes: Paper explicitly names piezoelectric contact microphone as contact sensing modality.

products.full_name: XRZero-G0: Pushing the Frontier of Dexterous Robotic Manipulation with Interfaces, Quality and Ratios
products.name: XRZero-G0
products.key_innovation: Wearable VR rig (PICO 4 + heterogeneous H/G grippers + backpack compute) with closed-loop Collection–Inspection–Training–Evaluation pipeline; empirical 10:1 robot-free/real-robot data mixing achieves comparable policy performance at ~1/20 acquisition cost

specs.main_camera: PICO 4 adjustable RGB top-view + dual wrist cameras (≥3 views)
specs.tracking_details: VR controller-mounted grippers; inter-gripper baseline calibrated to target dual-arm robot
specs.dataset: G0-Dataset (2,000+ hours, 3,000 tasks; XRZero-G0-3K subset on product page)

products.full_name: In-the-Wild Compliant Manipulation with UMI-FT
products.name: UMI-FT
products.key_innovation: Adds per-finger 6-axis force/torque sensing to a handheld UMI-style parallel-jaw gripper by mounting a coin-sized capacitive CoinFT sensor on each finger, with an iPhone supplying RGB, ultrawide RGB, depth and ARKit pose; the multimodal, robot-free demonstrations train an Adaptive Compliance Policy that predicts position targets, grasp force and stiffness for compliance controllers.

labs: Stanford Biomimetics and Dexterous Manipulation Lab (BDML)
specs.force_sensor: CoinFT — coin-sized capacitive 6-axis force/torque sensor, one per finger (per-finger wrench)
specs.sensing_device: iPhone (ARKit): RGB, ultrawide RGB, depth, 6-DoF pose
specs.gripper: Handheld parallel-jaw; real-world deployment on Universal Robots arm with WSG gripper
specs.policy: Adaptive Compliance Policy (extends Diffusion Policy), outputs position targets + grasp force + stiffness
specs.data_format: Zarr: RGB/depth video, force/torque, robot states, computed virtual-target trajectories

products.notes: Field corrections vs. submitted candidate (verified against primary sources): pose_tracking visual_slam->visual_inertial (iPhone ARKit is visual-inertial, matching original UMI); tactile_integration type_unspecified->none (CoinFT is a force/torque sensor, captured under force_torque_integration, not a separate tactile modality); commercialization_stage paper_only->oss_diy (hardware and software are open-sourced with full build/deploy instructions); bimanual kept as extensible (UMI-style architecture).

products.name: PXCap III
products.full_name: PXCap III Tri-Finger Data Collection Glove
products.key_innovation: 1:1 collection-execution isomorphism with PXDex III robotic hand; full tactile perception (fingertips, fingerpads, palm) via 6D Hall Array sensor; self-developed anti-magnetic encoders for high-precision joint capture

specs.tactile_sensor_name: PaXini 6D Hall Array Multidimensional Tactile Sensor
specs.tracking_details: VR Headset + Controller (≤5 mm wrist positioning precision); supports other positioning solutions

products.notes: Part of PaXini's full-body 6-axis F/T sensing ecosystem alongside PXDex III, DexH13/DexH5 dexterous hands, and TORA humanoid robots.

products.name: RoboPocket
products.full_name: RoboPocket: Improve Robot Policies Instantly with Your Phone
products.key_innovation: Robot-Free Instant Policy Iteration via AR Visual Foresight on a consumer smartphone; remote GPU inference with <150ms latency enables closed-loop online finetuning without physical robot deployment

specs.main_camera: iPhone Pro with custom fisheye lens mount
specs.tracking_details: iPhone Pro VIO + kinematic solving + AR rendering at 60Hz; 2.8mm position / 0.4° rotation error (single device)
specs.auxiliary_sensor: ESP32-based Bluetooth interface with magnetic encoder for gripper width

products.name: ART-Glove
products.full_name: ART-Glove: Articulated Tactile Glove for Contact-Grounded Dexterous Interaction Capture
products.key_innovation: 16 rigid functional surfaces with 22 anatomically aligned joints make hand-side contact geometry explicit; synchronized 22-DoF encoder joint motion and 2048-taxel piezoresistive tactile sensing at 120 Hz

specs.joint_angle_sensor: AS5600L magnetic rotary encoders (22 sensed DoFs)
specs.tracking_details: On-glove encoder-based joint sensing; sub-degree MAE across three joint mechanism types
specs.finger_joint_tracking: 22-DoF anatomically aligned (4 DoF × 4 fingers + 6 DoF thumb)

policy_standards.name: MIIT Intelligent Data Collection Standard 1.0
policy_standards.scope: synchronization, format, labeling, quality grading of multimodal robot training data

policy_standards.name: Shanghai Embodied Intelligence Plan
policy_standards.scope: China's first comprehensive plan, R&D + shared infrastructure

curated_content.title: Getting a Grip on Robotic Data Collection
curated_content.summary: Challenges in handheld data collection (transfer is hard when the robot gripper uses a different interface) and the Koala gripper approach of co-designing human and robot linkages. Covers sim-to-real, force sensing, and references to UMI.

companion_links.title: Handheld robotic data collection (RAI Institute blog)
